pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce phoenix-pubsub: a topic-based publish-subscribe system for asyncio applications, its design philosophy, and key features

pages.title: Installation and Requirements
pages.purpose: Guide users through installing phoenix-pubsub, including Python version requirements and the zero-dependency architecture

pages.title: Quick Start Guide
pages.purpose: Provide a rapid introduction with basic code examples showing subscribe, broadcast, and unsubscribe operations

pages.title: Core Concepts
pages.purpose: Explain the fundamental architecture and design patterns of the PubSub system

pages.title: The PubSub Class
pages.purpose: Detailed documentation of the PubSub class, its internal architecture, the topic registry, and concurrency control with asyncio.Lock

pages.title: Type System: Topics, Messages, and Peers
pages.purpose: Explain the type aliases and their roles: Topic (str), Message (Any), Peer (asyncio.Queue), Subscribers, and Dispatcher

pages.title: Subscription Management
pages.purpose: How subscribe() and unsubscribe() work, metadata attachment, and the subscription lifecycle

pages.title: Message Broadcasting
pages.purpose: Explain broadcast() and broadcast_from() methods, message delivery mechanics, and the two-phase lock strategy

pages.title: Custom Dispatchers
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the dispatcher mechanism, the synchronous_dispatcher implementation, the concurrent_dispatcher implementation, and creating custom routing logic

pages.title: Usage Patterns and Examples
pages.purpose: Demonstrate common usage patterns with practical code examples

pages.title: Basic Pub/Sub Pattern
pages.purpose: Simple subscribe-and-broadcast scenarios with multiple topics and subscribers

pages.title: Publisher Exclusion (Chat Pattern)
pages.purpose: Using broadcast_from() to exclude the sender from receiving their own messages

pages.title: Metadata-Based Filtering
pages.purpose: Implementing content-based routing using subscriber metadata and custom dispatchers

pages.title: Handling Slow Consumers
pages.purpose: Understanding message dropping behavior for queues at maxsize and backpressure handling

pages.title: Multi-Topic Broadcasting
pages.purpose: Broadcasting a single message to multiple topics simultaneously

pages.title: API Reference
pages.purpose: Complete API documentation for all public classes, methods, and type aliases

pages.title: Development Guide
pages.purpose: Guide for contributors: project structure, tooling, and development workflows

pages.title: Project Structure and Configuration
pages.purpose: Explain the repository layout, pyproject.toml configuration, and uv-based dependency management

pages.title: Build and Release Workflow
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide to versioning, building, tagging, and publishing releases using uv

pages.title: Documentation Generation
pages.purpose: How documentation is generated with pdoc, theme customization, and the CI/CD deployment pipeline

pages.title: Testing and Validation
pages.purpose: Overview of the test suite structure and how to run integration tests

pages.title: Advanced Topics
pages.purpose: In-depth coverage of advanced features and implementation details

pages.title: Concurrency and Thread Safety
pages.purpose: Detailed explanation of the asyncio.Lock strategy, the two-phase broadcast approach, and race condition prevention

pages.title: Asyncio Integration
pages.purpose: How phoenix-pubsub leverages asyncio.Queue, event loops, and async/await patterns

pages.title: Design Decisions and Trade-offs
pages.purpose: Explain architectural choices: zero dependencies, message dropping vs blocking, snapshot-based broadcasting

pages.title: Documentation System Internals
pages.purpose: For maintainers: how the documentation website works, including search and theming

pages.title: Search Functionality
pages.purpose: How the elasticlunr-based client-side search is implemented and indexed

pages.title: Theme System
pages.purpose: CSS architecture for light/dark themes and syntax highlighting

pages.title: CI/CD Pipeline for Docs
pages.purpose: GitHub Actions workflow for automated documentation builds and GitHub Pages deployment
